zh: 没有任何征兆，姜海就出现在新手岛的沙滩上了。
姜海踏上海岛的瞬间，炽热阳光裹挟咸润海风扑面而来。眼前展开一片原始而瑰丽的景象：翡翠色海浪轻吻着月牙状沙滩，远处峭壁如青铜巨盾矗立于碧空之下，岛屿中央蒸腾的水汽在阳光下幻化出七彩虹晕。
"这里很热，这里是哪里？难道又是个异界空间？"
环顾四周，也没看到人，但姜海的一身工作装和这片沙滩显得格格不入。
拖下外衣拿在手里，姜海仔细打量着周围情况：
金色沙滩绵延数公里，沙粒细软如糖霜，退潮时浪花在滩涂勾勒出银边，仿佛给碧海镶上闪亮裙裾。潮间带散落彩贝与鹅卵石，寄居蟹驮着螺壳横窜，留下竹叶状足迹。
【欢迎来到新手岛，这是宿主的第一个新手任务，比较简单，主要是让宿主适应任务流程】
【任务简介】
【海岛休闲之旅：抓螃蟹】
【任务描述】
【海岛上的海产很丰富，螃蟹数量众多，请愉快的捕捞吧。】
【小提示，有些螃蟹可不好对付，必要时要采取武力哦】
【任务目标】
【捕获5只螃蟹】
【任务进度】
姜海看完任务说明不由得皱了皱眉。
貌似新手任务不算太难，只要在这个海岛抓五只螃蟹即可，不过提示里说到必要时采取武力，不是废话吗？螃蟹会老老实实的呆着那边让你抓吗？
【系统面板自动开始切换】
姜海的脑海中视觉看到之前的界面发生了一些变化，
左下角的【生物列表】里的生物都变成了***星号，好像并不能探测周围生物。
视觉屏幕右方出现了一个人物装备栏界面，但都是空的。
人物装备栏下方是物品栏格子。目前有16个空白格子。
姜海可以随意的通过意念选中这些装备栏格子和物品栏格子，但没什么效果。
视觉屏幕左方是姜海目前属性栏，显示着：
【基础属性已激活】
【力量1  敏捷1  智力1  攻击力1  防御力1】
姜海脑海里的这些系统面板界面呈半透明状，仿佛他戴着一个VR立体3D眼镜那样。但比眼镜方便多了。
需要了解一下周围情况，姜海决定先调查清楚再行动。
"系统，我所处的海岛在哪里，是地球的热带海洋区域吗？"
【宿主您好，给你做一个简要的说明：】
【新手岛是一个独立空间，方圆数百公里，可以理解为一颗独立的星球的一块海洋区域】
【用户在这个独立空间的时间是单独计算，不影响现实时间】
【新手岛的停留时限是24小时，如果完不成任务将会被强制传送出去。】
【四周都是海水，除了这座岛是陆地外，没有其他陆地】
【这座岛是专为新手任务创建出来的，目前来新手岛的用户有数万人】
【平时普通游客也可以使用观光券来到岛上旅游】
【您的任务是抓螃蟹，只需要找到螃蟹后，抓住它，控制住5秒，即可收入到您的物品栏】
【物品栏已升级】
【当前物品栏格子温度是30度，您可以自行调节格子温度】
【小提示，螃蟹的适宜温度是 5摄氏度至 10摄氏度，冷冻负18度会导致螃蟹被冻死】
"这系统还真够贴心的，抓螃蟹的任务居然给我的物品栏提供调温？"
姜海感觉这个系统是不是还提供冷链服务，是不是抓到的螃蟹还能卖掉……
抛开胡思乱想，姜海开始东张西望的找起螃蟹来。
姜海以前也没去过沙滩，所以对海边如何找到螃蟹还真没经验。
在海滩搜索了一小会儿，姜海就有所发现了。
在不远处居然有一个黄色名字框出现。
走近一看，写着种类：螃蟹 名字：蟹标。顺着黄色名字框向下看，就见一只螃蟹在慢悠悠的横行着。像是在散步。
"细节啊，这就是细节！"姜海已经无语了。
黄色框一般标识中立，没伤害性，不会主动攻击别人。这是常识，都不需要系统解释。物种种类标出来，让人一目了然，甚至螃蟹都有名字，这个就细节到位了。别的不说，这个设计任务系统的人或其他什么高级生命体是走心的。
姜海看了看螃蟹。
二话不说，上去一把就把它按住。
螃蟹被突如其来的情况吓得拼命挣扎，但一只大手死死的按着它纹丝不动。
姜海看见手上有个红色进度圈在旋转，差不多五秒，感觉手一轻，螃蟹不见了。
与此同时，姜海脑海视觉里出现物品栏里多了一只螃蟹样的图标。姜海此时通过意念选中这个框，发现可以调整温度。立刻调成5摄氏度。
【成功抓到1只螃蟹，当前任务进度1/5】
【奖励：属性点+1，经验+10】
"嗯，新手任务不错，可惜看情况是不能重复接的任务，否则天天来抓，就是不知道这螃蟹能否卖钱……"
"系统，抓到的螃蟹可以卖钱吗？"
【可以】
en: Without warning, without fanfare—Jiang Hai was standing on a beach.

The moment his boots hit the sand, a blast of tropical heat wrapped around him, salted sea breeze slapping his face. Before him sprawled something straight out of a postcard: emerald waves lapping at a crescent shoreline, distant cliffs standing like bronze shields against a cloudless sky, and steam rising from the island's interior, fracturing sunlight into a shimmering rainbow haze.
*It's sweltering. Where the hell am I? Another pocket dimension?*
He scanned the shore. No one in sight. But his dark-blue factory overalls looked absolutely ridiculous against this tropical paradise.
He peeled off the jacket, slung it over one arm, and took a proper look around:

The beach stretched for miles—powder-fine sand, sugar-white, with the retreating tide tracing silver lace along the shore. In the intertidal zone, colorful shells and smooth pebbles lay scattered while hermit crabs scuttled sideways, hauling borrowed homes on their backs, leaving delicate tracks like fallen bamboo leaves.
[Ding]
[Welcome to Novice Island. This is your first beginner mission—primarily designed to familiarize you with mission protocols.]
[Mission Brief]
[Island Leisure Trip: Crab Catching]
[Mission Description]
[The island is rich in marine life and crabs are abundant. Enjoy your harvest.]
[Tip: Some crabs won't go down easy. Use force if necessary.]
[Mission Objective]
[Capture 5 crabs.]
[Mission Progress]
Jiang Hai finished reading and frowned.
On the surface, easy enough—just grab five crabs. But that "use force" tip? Come on. Like crabs were just gonna sit there and wait politely to be picked up?
[Ding]
[System interface reconfiguration in progress.]
The display in Jiang Hai's mind flickered as panels shifted and rearranged.
The [Biological Signatures] list in the lower-left now showed only *** asterisks. Apparently, creature detection was disabled here.
On the right side of his visual field, a character equipment panel materialized. Completely empty.
Below it: an inventory grid. Sixteen blank slots.
Jiang Hai could select them mentally, focus on each slot... but nothing happened. Just empty boxes.
On the left: his stat panel.
[Base Attributes Activated]
[STR 1 | AGI 1 | INT 1 | ATK 1 | DEF 1]
All these system panels floated in his vision semi-transparently, like wearing a pair of VR goggles hardwired into his brain. Way more convenient than actual hardware, he had to admit.
*Let's get the lay of the land first. Scope things out, then move.*
"System: where exactly is this island? Is it in Earth's tropical zone?"
[Host, a brief orientation:]
[Novice Island is an independent pocket space spanning several hundred square kilometers. Think of it as an isolated oceanic region on its own miniature planet.]
[Time within this space runs independently and does not affect real-world time.]
[Maximum stay on Novice Island: 24 hours. Failure to complete the mission will result in forced teleportation.]
[The island is surrounded entirely by ocean. No other landmasses exist within this space.]
[This island was generated specifically for novice missions. Current concurrent users on Novice Island: several tens of thousands.]
[Regular tourists can also visit via sightseeing passes.]
[Your mission: locate crabs, capture them, and maintain physical control for 5 seconds. They will then be deposited into your inventory.]
[Inventory has been upgraded.]
[Current inventory slot temperature: 30°C. You may adjust slot temperatures at will.]
[Tip: Optimal crab storage temperature is 5°C to 10°C. Freezing at -18°C will kill the crabs.]
*This system is absurdly thoughtful. Temperature-controlled inventory slots... for catching crabs?*
Jiang Hai couldn't help wondering if the system also offered cold-chain logistics. Could he sell these crabs after catching them?
He shook off the random thoughts and started scanning the beach for crabs.
Thing was, Jiang Hai had never been to a beach in his life. He had zero experience finding crabs by the sea.
Didn't take long, though. Something caught his eye.
A yellow nameplate, floating in the air a short distance away.

He walked closer. The label read: [Species: Crab | Name: Crab Mark]. Following the yellow nameplate downward, he spotted it—a crab, taking a leisurely sideways stroll like it was out for an evening walk.
*The attention to detail! Unreal.* Jiang Hai was speechless.

Yellow markers: neutral, non-aggressive, won't attack unless provoked. RPG 101—no system tutorial needed. But species labels? And the crabs had actual names? Someone—or some higher being—had put serious thought into designing this mission system.
Jiang Hai eyeballed the crab.
No words. No warning. He lunged and pinned it.
The crab went into full panic mode, legs thrashing wildly, claws snapping at air—but a human hand had it locked down. Zero movement.
A red progress ring appeared over his hand, spinning clockwise. About five seconds later, his grip went weightless. The crab vanished.
Simultaneously, a crab icon popped into one of his inventory slots. Jiang Hai focused on it mentally—temperature controls available. He immediately dialed it down to 5°C.
[Ding]
[1 crab captured. Current mission progress: 1/5.]
[Reward: Attribute Points +1 | EXP +10]
"Not bad for a tutorial mission. Too bad it looks like a one-time deal—otherwise I'd be here every day. Question is: can I actually sell these crabs...?"
"System. Can I sell the crabs I catch?"
[Ding]
[Yes.]
Jiang Hai: "......"
